title: Future Perfect Continuous
definition: The Future Perfect Continuous tense emphasizes the duration of an action that will continue up until a specific point in the future.
formula: Subject + will have been + V-ing + Object

structure.positive: By next month, I will have been working here for five years.
structure.negative: By next month, I will not have been working here long.
structure.question: Will you have been working here for five years by next month?
structure.whQuestion: How long will you have been working here by next month?

usage: Use Future Perfect Continuous to emphasize how long something will have continued by a certain future time: By December, she will have been studying English for two years.
examples: By next month, I will have been working here for five years.
By the time she graduates, she will have been studying for four years.
In June, they will have been living in this city for a decade.
By 10 PM, we will have been driving for six hours.
Will you have been working here for ten years by 2027?
By the end of this year, he will have been coding for a decade.

dailyConversation.speaker: A
dailyConversation.line: How long will you have been working here by next year?

dailyConversation.speaker: B
dailyConversation.line: I will have been working here for five years by then.

commonMistakes.wrong: I will have work here for five years.
commonMistakes.right: I will have been working here for five years.
commonMistakes.why: Include 'been' and use V-ing to emphasize duration.

commonMistakes.wrong: By June, she will studying for two years.
commonMistakes.right: By June, she will have been studying for two years.
commonMistakes.why: Don't drop 'have been' from the formula.

quiz.question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: I will have been working for years.
I will have work for years.
I will been working for years.
I have will been working for years.
quiz.answer: I will have been working for years.

quiz.question: By 2028, they ___ (live) here for 20 years.
quiz.explanation: Emphasizes duration up to a future point.
